entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: the sphota-self is not divided by them

entities.name: prakṛta-dhvani
entities.cite: Contrastive sound-properties (quantity, the phonemic pattern) belong to the prakrta dhvani and determine which sphota is manifested

entities.name: vaikṛta-dhvani
entities.cite: the vaikrta dhvanayah, modified sounds, which carry vrtti-bheda, the differences of delivery (the tradition enumerates drutā, madhyamā, vilambitā — fast, medium, slow)

entities.name: vṛtti-bheda
entities.cite: which carry vrtti-bheda, the differences of delivery

entities.name: manifestation-duration
entities.cite: determine only how long the manifesting takes

axioms.name: vahate
axioms.cite: the vaikrta dhvanayah, modified sounds, which carry vrtti-bheda, the differences of delivery (the tradition enumerates drutā, madhyamā, vilambitā — fast, medium, slow)

axioms.name: samarthayate
axioms.cite: Contrastive sound-properties (quantity, the phonemic pattern) belong to the prakrta dhvani and determine which sphota is manifested

axioms.name: avisambhedya
axioms.cite: The sphota itself — tair na bhidyate — is divided by neither

axioms.name: avaisambhadika
axioms.cite: These affect nothing criterial: the same word said quickly is the same word

denials.name: bhidyate
denials.cite: The sphota itself — tair na bhidyate — is divided by neither

claims.name: vahate
claims.cite: the modified sounds bring in the difference of speed of delivery

claims.name: avisambhedya
claims.cite: the sphota-self is not divided by them

rejected_readings.label: sphota_divided_by_vaikrta
rejected_readings.rendering: The modified sounds divide or differentiate the sphota into distinct essences.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly denies this: the sphota is not divided by the vaikrta dhvani. The non-contrastive sounds only affect the duration of manifestation, not the underlying essence.

rejected_readings.label: tempo_determines_sphota
rejected_readings.rendering: The differences in speed of delivery determine which word (sphota) is manifested.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary establishes a clear division of labor: the contrastive properties (prakrta dhvani—quantity, phonemic pattern) determine which sphota manifests, while the tempo differences (vrtti-bheda) only affect the duration of manifestation, not which word is produced.

rejected_readings.label: prakrta_vaikrta_equivalence
rejected_readings.rendering: All sound differences, whether related to quantity or tempo, work together uniformly to determine and manifest the sphota.
rejected_readings.why: The entire explanatory power of the commentary depends on distinguishing prakrta (contrastive: quantity, phonemic pattern) from vaikrta (non-contrastive: tempo, voice) dhvani. Prakrta determines WHICH sphota; vaikrta determines only HOW LONG manifestation takes. This reading collapses that critical distinction and loses the phonemic/phonetic insight.

notes: The contract encodes Bhartṛhari's phonemic/phonetic distinction as the division between prakṛta (contrastive, sphota-determining) and vaikṛta (non-contrastive, duration-affecting) dhvani. The sphota's indivisibility is the core: neither class of sound-properties divides it; they differ only in what aspect of manifestation they govern.
